headline: Public Health Workforce Vacancy Rate Hits 18% Across All Categories
context: MOH faces critical staffing shortages with nearly 1 in 5 public health positions unfilled nationwide.

cards.big: 18% vacancy rate across all MOH positions leaves 28,000 public health jobs unfilled in 2025
cards.sub: Pharmacists medical laboratory technologists and health inspectors face worst shortages

cards.big: Public sector health salaries average 35% below private sector for equivalent qualifications
cards.sub: Wage competition from private healthcare and overseas opportunities drives attrition

cards.big: Rural posting incentives of RM500-1,000 monthly have not been increased since 2012
cards.sub: 13-year-old incentive rates are ineffective against current cost-of-living pressures

cards.big: Botswana reduced health worker vacancy from 25% to 8% through rural service bonuses tripled in 2020
cards.sub: Meaningful financial incentives proportional to hardship attracted staff to underserved areas

cards.big: A health system that cannot fill 1 in 5 positions is not managing vacancies—it is managing collapse
Should public health salaries be benchmarked against private sector rates to close the vacancy gap
